text: How are diagrams such as percent bars (tape diagrams) or double number lines helpful for answering percent questions? What other strategies might be helpful?

Students make connections between and among a variety of strategies for solving problems involving percents.

Students look closely at a variety of percent problem-solving strategies to notice that

  • tape diagrams and double number lines allow you to easily visualize the size of a percentage;
  • ratio tables, unit rates, scaling, and 10 × 10 area grids can each be useful in solving some rate problems; and
  • while many strategies may be used to solve percent problems, some may be more or less efficiently applied than others and all strategies should be considered.

Students solve a variety of percent problems, including those asking students to find

  • the percent given a ratio;
  • the quantity in the whole given the ratio and amount in the part of the whole; and
  • the quantity that is the part of the whole given the percent and amount in the whole.
